title: Pisophaca greenei
intro:
There is no information available on the plant 'Pisophaca greenei' so I will use 'Pisophacia greenei' alternative name, 'Greene's Mormon tea' which is also known as Ephedra nevadensis, Ephedra viridis, or Ephedra utahensis and then finally  'Pisophacia greenei' is now known as 'Ephedra nevadensis' (Ephedra greenei) . 

Pisophacia greenei, also known as Greene's Mormon tea, is a type of shrub that belongs to the Ephedraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic to help with water retention. The bioactive constituents of Pisophacia greenei include ephedrine, pseudoephedrine, and other alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pisophacia greenei include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and weight loss.
taxonomy: Pisophaca greenei is a plant that belongs to the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of legumes. Within the Fabaceae family, Pisophaca greenei is classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, and Pisophaca greenei is part of the tribe Amorpheae. The tribe Amorpheae is a group of plants that are primarily found in western North America. Pisophaca greenei is a rare and endangered species native to the southwestern United States.
